paper_title: Aerobiological Study of Hazel Pollen in Lublin

methods.description: Process for collecting airborne hazel pollen data using a Lanzoni VPPS 2000 sampler and analyzing it microscopically to determine pollen concentration.

steps.description: The aerobiological study was conducted in Lublin in the years 2001-2009. For airborne hazel pollen counts, a Lanzoni VPPS 2000 sampler was used, which was placed on the roof of a building at a height of 18 m above ground level.
steps.searchable_summary: set up and operate Lanzoni VPPS 2000 sampler for pollen collection

steps.description: Microscopic analysis of the pollen collected on the tape was performed on 4 horizontal sweeps of the slide.
steps.searchable_summary: perform microscopic analysis on pollen samples

steps.description: Pollen concentration was expressed as the mean daily number of pollen grains per 1 m3 of air.

methods.description: Collection of meteorological data to estimate the influence of meteorological factors on the start date of the hazel pollen season and statistical analysis using multiple regression.

steps.description: Meteorological data came from the Meteorological Observatory of the Meteorology and Climatology Department.

To estimate the influence of meteorological factors on the start date of the hazel pollen season, the following meteorological data were used: mean (t mean), minimum (t min) and maximum (t max) daily air temperature, mean daily relative humidity (f), daily total precipitation (p), mean daily cloud cover (n) and mean daily wind speed (v).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze meteorological data for influence on pollen season start

steps.description: In order to develop the statistical model, the multiple regression method was applied.
steps.searchable_summary: apply multiple regression to develop statistical model
